questions.questionText: The rate-limiting enzyme of de novo purine synthesis is:
questions.explanation: De novo purine synthesis: PRPP + glutamine → phosphoribosylamine (by GPAT — committed step). Inhibited by: AMP, GMP, IMP (feedback). Activated by: PRPP. Purine ring is built on ribose-5-phosphate. Sources of atoms: glycine (C4,C5,N7), glutamine (N3,N9), aspartate (N1), CO2 (C6), N10-formyl-THF (C2,C8). IMP is the first complete purine nucleotide → AMP or GMP. Reference: Harper's — Purine Synthesis.

questions.questionText: Lesch-Nyhan syndrome is caused by deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Lesch-Nyhan syndrome: HGPRT deficiency (X-linked recessive) → cannot salvage hypoxanthine/guanine → increased de novo purine synthesis → excess uric acid. Features: hyperuricemia, gout, intellectual disability, self-mutilation (lip and finger biting — pathognomonic), choreoathetosis, spasticity. Partial HGPRT deficiency → Kelley-Seegmiller syndrome (gout without neurological features). HGPRT is a purine salvage pathway enzyme. Reference: Harper's — Lesch-Nyhan.

questions.questionText: Gout is caused by deposition of:
questions.optionB: Monosodium urate (MSU) crystals in joints and tissues
questions.explanation: Gout: hyperuricemia → MSU crystal deposition in joints (especially 1st MTP — podagra), soft tissues (tophi), and kidneys (urate nephropathy, stones). Uric acid = end product of purine catabolism (by xanthine oxidase: hypoxanthine → xanthine → uric acid). Allopurinol: xanthine oxidase inhibitor (reduces urate production). Febuxostat: newer XO inhibitor. Colchicine: anti-inflammatory (microtubule inhibitor). Uricosurics: probenecid (increases renal excretion). Reference: Harper's — Gout.

questions.questionText: The rate-limiting enzyme of de novo pyrimidine synthesis is:
questions.explanation: De novo pyrimidine synthesis: CPS-II (rate-limiting, cytoplasmic) — glutamine + CO2 + 2ATP → carbamoyl phosphate. Different from CPS-I (mitochondrial, urea cycle). Pyrimidine ring is synthesized first then attached to ribose (opposite to purines). In mammals, first 3 enzymes (CPS-II + ATCase + DHOase) are on a single trifunctional protein: CAD. UMP → UTP → CTP. Feedback inhibition: UTP inhibits CPS-II. Reference: Harper's — Pyrimidine Synthesis.

ADA deficiency → deoxyadenosine accumulates → dATP accumulates → inhibits ribonucleotide reductase → blocks DNA synthesis in ALL cells, especially lymphocytes (rapidly dividing) → SCID (both T and B cell deficiency). Most common cause of autosomal recessive SCID. Purine nucleoside phosphorylase (PNP) deficiency → primarily T cell deficiency. Treatment: PEG-ADA enzyme replacement, gene therapy (first approved gene therapy), bone marrow transplant. Reference: Harper's — ADA Deficiency.

questions.questionText: 5-Fluorouracil (5-FU) acts as an anticancer drug by inhibiting:
questions.explanation: 5-FU: pyrimidine analog → converted to 5-FdUMP (fluorodeoxyuridine monophosphate) → forms stable covalent complex with thymidylate synthase and N5,N10-methylene-THF → irreversibly inhibits thymidylate synthase (suicide inhibition) → blocks dTMP synthesis → 'thymineless death' → inhibits DNA synthesis. Used in: colorectal, breast, head & neck cancers. Leucovorin (folinic acid) enhances 5-FU efficacy. Reference: Harper's — 5-FU.

questions.questionText: Hereditary orotic aciduria is caused by deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Hereditary orotic aciduria: AR, deficiency of UMP synthase (bifunctional enzyme: OPRT + OMP decarboxylase) → cannot convert orotic acid to UMP → orotic acid accumulates in urine. Features: megaloblastic anemia (not responsive to B12/folate, responds to uridine), growth retardation, orotic acid crystals in urine. Treatment: oral uridine (bypasses the block). Also elevated orotic acid in OTC deficiency (carbamoyl phosphate diverted). Reference: Harper's — Orotic Aciduria.

Ribonucleotide reductase (RNR): catalyzes reduction of ribonucleoside diphosphates (NDP) → deoxyribonucleoside diphosphates (dNDP). Essential for DNA synthesis. Converts: ADP→dADP, GDP→dGDP, CDP→dCDP, UDP→dUDP. Requires thioredoxin/glutaredoxin as electron donors. Allosterically regulated: ATP activates, dATP inhibits (ADA deficiency → dATP accumulation → inhibits RNR). Hydroxyurea inhibits RNR (used in sickle cell disease, CML). Reference: Harper's — RNR.

questions.questionText: Allopurinol is used in gout because it:
questions.optionB: Inhibits xanthine oxidase → decreases uric acid production
questions.explanation: Allopurinol: structural analog of hypoxanthine → competitive inhibitor of xanthine oxidase (XO). Also converted to alloxanthine (oxypurinol) by XO → irreversible inhibitor (suicide substrate). Decreases uric acid production → prevents gout attacks and urate nephropathy. Side effects: allopurinol hypersensitivity syndrome (HLA-B*5801). 6-MP and azathioprine are metabolized by XO → allopurinol potentiates their toxicity (reduce dose 75%). Febuxostat: non-purine XO inhibitor. Reference: Harper's — Allopurinol.

questions.questionText: The purine salvage pathway enzyme APRT (adenine phosphoribosyltransferase) converts:
questions.explanation: Purine salvage: recycles free purine bases → nucleotides (energy-efficient alternative to de novo synthesis). APRT: adenine + PRPP → AMP. HGPRT: hypoxanthine + PRPP → IMP, and guanine + PRPP → GMP. Salvage is especially important in brain and RBCs (cannot perform de novo synthesis). Salvage pathway defects: HGPRT deficiency → Lesch-Nyhan. APRT deficiency → 2,8-dihydroxyadenine kidney stones. Reference: Harper's — Purine Salvage.

questions.questionText: The end product of purine catabolism in humans is:
questions.explanation: Purine catabolism: AMP → IMP → inosine → hypoxanthine → (xanthine oxidase) → xanthine → (xanthine oxidase) → uric acid. GMP → guanosine → guanine → xanthine → uric acid. Uric acid is the end product in humans (we lack uricase/urate oxidase which converts uric acid → allantoin in most other mammals). This is why humans are susceptible to gout. Rasburicase (recombinant urate oxidase) used for tumor lysis syndrome. Reference: Harper's — Purine Catabolism.

questions.questionText: Azathioprine is an immunosuppressant prodrug that is converted to:
questions.optionB: 6-mercaptopurine (6-MP, a purine analog that inhibits de novo purine synthesis)
questions.explanation: Azathioprine → 6-MP (in vivo) → 6-thio-IMP (by HGPRT) → inhibits de novo purine synthesis (feedback inhibition) and is incorporated into DNA → immunosuppression (inhibits lymphocyte proliferation). Used in: organ transplant rejection prevention, autoimmune diseases (SLE, IBD, RA). 6-MP is also metabolized by XO → allopurinol interaction (reduce 6-MP dose 75%). TPMT (thiopurine methyltransferase) polymorphism affects toxicity. Reference: Harper's — Azathioprine/6-MP.
